reason_thou:
1. Understanding the Issue:
The IPSCCP pass replaced an fcmp une with a constant splat true inside a function with attribute "denormal-fp-math"="ieee,dynamic". This is incorrect because under dynamic denormal handling, denormal inputs are treated as unknown at compile time; they may be flushed to zero or kept as IEEE denormals at runtime. Specifically, comparing a denormal value against zero using UNE can be true (IEEE) or false (flush-to-zero), so folding to true is unsound.

2. Analyzing opt's Log:
The SCCP debug output shows it merged a constant <2 x i1> splat(true) into the fcmp. Alive2 demonstrates a counterexample with lane 0 false and lane 1 true, which is legal under dynamic denormal handling, hence the transformation is incorrect.

3. Root Cause Analysis:
The first transformation is triggered by SCCPSolver::tryToReplaceWithConstant, which uses SCCPInstVisitor::getConstantOrNull. The constant came from SCCPInstVisitor::visitCmpInst, which calls ValueLatticeElement::getCompare(..., DL) to fold comparisons.

ValueLatticeElement::getCompare uses ConstantFoldCompareInstOperands(Pred, C0, C1, DL) without providing the instruction context. In ConstantFolding.cpp, denormal-aware folding relies on FlushFPConstant(Operand, I, ...) which needs the Instruction* to query the function's denormal-fp-math mode. If I is null, FlushFPConstant becomes a no-op. Consequently, folding proceeds as if denormals are IEEE, incorrectly producing true for (denorm != 0.0).

Thus, SCCP/ValueLattice missed passing the Instruction* to constant folding, so denormal dynamic modes were ignored.

4. Proposed Edit Point(s):
- Edit Point 1: llvm/include/llvm/Analysis/ValueLattice.h (around declaration of getCompare)
  Change the declaration of ValueLatticeElement::getCompare to accept an optional const Instruction* parameter with a default of nullptr. Also ensure Instruction is forward-declared in this header, since it only includes Constants.h/ConstantRange today.
  Rationale: This allows callers that have instruction context (like SCCP) to provide it so ConstantFolding can respect denormal modes.

- Edit Point 2: llvm/lib/Analysis/ValueLattice.cpp (file header untouched; ensure includes are sufficient)
  Adjust the definition signature of ValueLatticeElement::getCompare to take the new const Instruction *I parameter. Update the internal call to ConstantFoldCompareInstOperands to pass I through: ConstantFoldCompareInstOperands(Pred, getConstant(), Other.getConstant(), DL, /*TLI=*/nullptr, I). No other behavioral change.
  Rationale: Propagates instruction context to denormal-aware constant folding.

- Edit Point 3: llvm/lib/Analysis/ValueLattice.cpp (actual function body lines 14-30 as shown) 
  Replace the existing getCompare definition with the new one including the Instruction* parameter and forwarding it. The rest of the function logic remains the same so integer range comparisons and equality-not-constant cases continue to work.

- Edit Point 4: llvm/lib/Transforms/Utils/SCCPSolver.cpp (visitCmpInst at ~1607)
  Update the call to getCompare to pass &I (the CmpInst) as the instruction context:
    Constant *C = V1State.getCompare(I.getPredicate(), I.getType(), V2State, DL, &I);
  Rationale: Ensures SCCP's folding of compares is denormal-mode aware. With "ieee,dynamic" and a denormal constant, ConstantFoldCompareInstOperands will return nullptr (FlushFPConstant returns nullptr for dynamic denormal mode), preventing SCCP from incorrectly marking the compare as a constant.

Notes:
- Other call sites (LazyValueInfo, FunctionSpecialization) can continue to call the method without the new parameter; the default nullptr preserves current behavior. If desired, they can be incrementally updated later wherever an instruction is available.
- We add a forward declaration of class Instruction in ValueLattice.h to avoid pulling in heavy headers.

5. Conclusion:
By threading the instruction context through ValueLatticeElement::getCompare and using it in SCCP's visitCmpInst, constant folding of floating-point comparisons will honor denormal-fp-math modes. Under ieee,dynamic, folding of comparisons with denormal constants now returns nullptr (unknown), preventing ipsccp from replacing the compare with a constant. This fixes the incorrect transformation observed in the reproducer.
